mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dragonfly is a cloud-native project that provides efficient, stable, and secure data distribution and acceleration using P2P technology. Installing Dragonfly allows you to improve the delivery of files, container images, and other artifacts in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflyoss.github.io/dragonfly/charts
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your local Helm chart repository cache to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
Install Dragonfly in a new namespace called 'dragonfly' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --version v2.4.1 --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Dragonfly pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace dragonfly
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Dragonfly deployment to set resource limits for better performance:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment dragonfly --namespace dragonfly
```
Add the following under `spec.containers.resources`:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Dragonfly will show the Dragonfly pods in the 'dragonfly' namespace as running. You should also have configured resource limits for optimal performance.
codeSnippets: helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --version v2.4.1 --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
